hero.headline: Vignan Online MBA in Finance: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A+ accredited | Rs 90,000 flat fee

sections.tldr: Vignan Online MBA in Finance covers Management of Banking and Financial Services, Direct and Indirect Taxation, Fixed Income Securities and Derivatives, Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management, Fintech Foundation and Applications, and Corporate Valuation and Investment Banking. Total fee Rs 90,000 flat. Zero-cost EMI Rs 5,700/month directly through the university. NAAC A+ accredited. NIRF #70 University.

about.body:
Vignan's Online MBA in Finance covers both traditional finance (banking management, taxation, fixed income, portfolio management) and contemporary finance (fintech applications, corporate valuation, investment banking). At Rs 90,000 total, this is among the most affordable NAAC A+ online MBA finance programmes in India.

The inclusion of Fintech Foundation and Applications as a standalone Sem 4 subject is notable at this fee point. Most sub-Rs 1L online MBAs cover fintech as a module, not a dedicated course. Corporate Valuation and Investment Banking as another standalone Sem 4 subject adds depth for candidates targeting BFSI and advisory roles.

Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research is a deemed university in Guntur, Andhra Pradesh. NAAC A+ accredited, NIRF #70 University 2025, UGC-DEB approved. The university offers zero-cost EMI directly (Rs 5,700/month), with no external NBFC or marketing partner involved.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core (Principles of Management, Managerial Economics, Accounting for Managers, Business Statistics, Legal Environment in Sem 1; Marketing Management, Financial Markets and Corporate Finance, HRM, Operations Management, Business Research Methods, Business Analytics in Sem 2). Semesters 3-4 below show the Finance specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Business Environment and Strategy
subjects.desc: Macro-economic environment analysis, industry structure, competitive strategy, and strategic planning frameworks.

subjects.name: Management of Banking and Financial Services
subjects.desc: Banking operations, NBFC regulation, insurance sector, mutual fund mechanics, and financial services distribution channels.

subjects.name: Direct and Indirect Taxation
subjects.desc: Income tax provisions, GST framework, TDS compliance, advance tax planning, and corporate tax strategy.

subjects.name: Fixed Income Securities and Derivatives
subjects.desc: Bond valuation, yield curve analysis, options and futures pricing, hedging strategies, and derivative risk management.

subjects.name: Research-Based Learning-I
subjects.desc: Guided research project on a finance topic. Literature review, methodology design, and initial data collection.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business plan creation, funding strategies, and entrepreneurial risk management.

subjects.name: Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management
subjects.desc: Equity valuation (DCF, PE, PBV), Modern Portfolio Theory, CAPM, portfolio construction, and risk-adjusted return metrics.

subjects.name: Fintech Foundation and Applications
subjects.desc: Digital payments (UPI), blockchain in banking, robo-advisory platforms, regtech, and AI-driven credit scoring.

subjects.name: Corporate Valuation and Investment Banking
subjects.desc: DCF valuation, comparable company analysis, M&A transaction structuring, LBO basics, and deal advisory process.

subjects.name: Research-Based Learning-II
subjects.desc: Continuation of research project. Data analysis, findings presentation, and thesis completion.

whoHires.body:
Vignan MBA Finance graduates target roles across banking, corporate finance, and advisory:

**Industries:** Commercial banking, NBFC, insurance, corporate treasury, equity research, fintech, financial consulting, and government finance

**Entry-level roles:** Financial Analyst, Credit Analyst, Banking Officer, Tax Analyst, Equity Research Associate, Treasury Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Finance Manager, Credit Manager, Branch Manager (banking), Portfolio Analyst, Tax Manager, Financial Controller

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CFO (mid-market), VP Finance, Head of Treasury, Director of Credit Risk, Head of Corporate Finance

**Employers:** Banks (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, Axis), NBFCs (Bajaj Finance, Muthoot), insurance (LIC, HDFC Life), IT/ITES finance teams, and Andhra Pradesh/Telangana regional corporates.

**Note:** Vignan provides career support. Guntur/AP location provides South India regional access.

skills.body:
The Finance specialisation builds skills across capital markets, taxation, and fintech:

**Technical skills:**
- Investment Analysis: equity valuation (DCF, comparable), portfolio construction, risk-adjusted return calculation (Sharpe, Treynor)
- Derivatives: options pricing basics, futures hedging design, interest rate swap mechanics, and derivative risk measurement
- Taxation: income tax computation, GST return filing process, TDS compliance, and corporate tax planning
- Fintech: digital payments architecture, blockchain in banking, robo-advisory evaluation, and AI credit scoring interpretation

**Applied skills:**
- Corporate Valuation: DCF model building, comparable analysis, M&A premium analysis, and deal structuring basics
- Banking Operations: credit appraisal process, NPA management, and financial services product design

**Soft skills:** Presenting investment theses to committees, negotiating credit terms, managing audit relationships, and communicating financial risk to non-finance stakeholders.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Vignan Online MBA Finance against peer programmes:

**vs SMU Online MBA Finance:** SMU at Rs 1,20,000 is Rs 30,000 more. SMU has Manipal group brand and established placement infrastructure. Vignan at Rs 90,000 is cheaper with fintech and corporate valuation as standalone subjects. SMU wins on brand. Vignan wins on fee and contemporary finance depth.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Finance:** Galgotias at Rs 86,400 all-in is Rs 3,600 cheaper. Both are budget-tier with NAAC A+. Galgotias has Master Thesis structure. Vignan has fintech and investment banking depth. Near-identical tier with different curriculum strengths.

**vs IGNOU Online MBA Financial Management:** IGNOU at Rs 66,000 is the cheapest option with NAAC A++. IGNOU has no active LMS and no placement support. Vignan at Rs 90,000 adds Rs 24,000 for active LMS, zero-cost direct EMI, and fintech/valuation subjects. For candidates who need LMS and contemporary content, Vignan is the step up.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA Financial Management:** Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 (one-time after Early Bird) is near-identical fee. Both NAAC A+. Uttaranchal has Corporate Financial Management and Wealth Management. Vignan has Fintech and Corporate Valuation/Investment Banking. Choose based on curriculum preference.

fees.body:
The Vignan Online MBA in Finance is priced at Rs 90,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year programme). This makes Vignan Online among the most affordable UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research starting Rs 5,700 per month. No external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary involved.

**Additional charges**: Registration, application, examination, and project fees are not separately documented on Vignan Online's portal. Please reconfirm all charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Vignan Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and all applicable charges with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Finance as the specialisation, issued by Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #70 University rank, and UGC-DEB approval. Valid for government recruitment, PSU finance roles, banking promotions, and private-sector corporate finance positions. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Vignan Online MBA Finance students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Rajesh K.
items.liked: Rs 90,000 total for a NAAC A+ finance MBA with fintech and corporate valuation subjects. Cannot find this combination cheaper anywhere except IGNOU
items.disliked: Vignan brand recognition outside Andhra Pradesh and Telangana is limited. North India employers may not recognise the name immediately
items.body: I chose Vignan because the fee is unbeatable for a NAAC A+ deemed university. The fintech and fixed income subjects were directly applicable to my banking role. Zero-cost EMI at Rs 5,700/month made it accessible. The LMS is functional. The main trade-off is brand recognition. In AP and Telangana, Vignan is well-known. Nationally, the brand is still growing.

items.name: Priya S.
items.liked: Investment Analysis and Corporate Valuation subjects covered DCF and M&A at a depth I did not expect from a Rs 90,000 programme
items.disliked: Placement support for online MBA students could be more proactive. Self-directed job search is the reality
items.body: The finance curriculum is comprehensive for the price. Banking management, taxation, derivatives, portfolio management, fintech, and valuation in one programme. The research-based learning across Sem 3-4 forced structured thinking. Career services exist but are not as developed as larger universities.

items.name: Anil M.
items.liked: Direct and Indirect Taxation covered both income tax and GST at a practical level useful for my CA-adjacent work
items.disliked: Some recorded lectures felt dated. The LMS interface could be more modern. Study materials need updating for 2024-era financial regulations
items.body: At Rs 90,000 the value is clear. The curriculum covers what you need for a finance career. The delivery quality is adequate, not premium. Faculty engagement varies by subject. If you are in AP/Telangana and need an affordable NAAC A+ MBA, Vignan works. If brand recognition nationally is your priority, consider spending more.

faqs.question: Is Rs 90,000 the total Vignan Online MBA fee?
faqs.answer: Yes. Rs 90,000 is the total programme fee across 4 semesters. Registration, exam, and project fees are not separately documented on the portal. Reconfirm all charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

faqs.question: Does Vignan offer zero-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zero-cost EMI is available directly through the university starting Rs 5,700 per month. No external NBFC or marketing partner is involved in the financing.

faqs.question: What is Vignan's NAAC grade?
faqs.answer: NAAC A+. This is a strong accreditation grade. Among online MBA providers at the sub-Rs 1L fee point, NAAC A+ is a genuine differentiator. IGNOU holds NAAC A++ but at a different programme structure.

faqs.question: Does the Finance MBA cover fintech?
faqs.answer: Yes. Fintech Foundation and Applications is a standalone Semester 4 subject covering digital payments, blockchain in banking, robo-advisory, and AI-driven credit scoring. This is uncommon at the sub-Rs 1L fee point.

faqs.question: Is the Vignan online MBA valid for banking promotions?
faqs.answer: Yes. UGC-DEB approved online MBA is legally equivalent to campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines. Valid for IBPS, SBI PO, RBI Grade B, and PSU finance officer recruitment.

chooseIf: Rs 90,000 flat fee is your primary criterion. This is among the most affordable NAAC A+ online MBAs.
Fintech and Corporate Valuation as standalone subjects matter for your BFSI career.
Zero-cost EMI directly through the university (Rs 5,700/month) fits your budget.
You are in South India (AP/Telangana) where the Vignan brand carries recognition.
consider: National brand recognition matters. Amity, NMIMS, and MUJ have stronger pan-India MBA brand positioning.
IGNOU at Rs 66,000 is even cheaper with NAAC A++ but no active LMS or fintech content.
Galgotias at Rs 86,400 is marginally cheaper with NCR location advantage.
If premium placement support is needed, larger universities (Amity, CU, LPU) have bigger career services teams.
